zotero.abstractNote: We study the loss landscape of deep neural networks with ReLU activation functions. We show that for a class of deep neural networks, which includes networks with one hidden layer and networks with specific architectures, the loss function has no bad local valleys. A bad local valley is a local minimum that is not a global minimum. Our results provide a theoretical justification for the empirical observation that deep neural networks can be trained to global optimality using simple gradient-based optimization methods.
